title: Hope Well And Have Well: Or, Fair After Foul Weather.
content:
What though the heaven be lowering now,
    And look with a contracted brow?
    We shall discover, by-and-by,
    A repurgation of the sky;
    And when those clouds away are driven,
    Then will appear a cheerful heaven.
meaning:
"Hope Well And Have Well: Or, Fair After Foul Weather." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
